Included Features: Welcome surface; pre-auth CTAs
Description: Shows a marketing-style Landing screen before authentication with Get Started and Sign In CTAs.
User Value: Lower friction for first-time visitors than dropping directly onto Login.
Implementation Detail: App.tsx initial screen is landing; LandingScreen calls goToLogin for both CTAs.
Current Status Notes: Marketing copy is local; no analytics/AB testing yet.

Included Features: Email/password login; logout
Description: Provides a branded login entry point and a local logout path from the authenticated app. The login form includes email, password, password visibility toggle, submit button, and social login placeholders.
User Value: Lets users enter the app, switch accounts, or exit their session.
Implementation Detail: LoginScreen calls onLoginSuccess; App.tsx routes to onboarding or home. handleLogout resets isLoggedIn and returns to Landing.
Current Status Notes: Demo-only auth; logout is wired locally. No API auth request, token, session refresh, or persisted login state.

Included Features: Welcome flow; location setup; skill setup
Description: Guides first-time users through setup with a welcome step, city/ZIP input, and skill-level selector from 2.0 to 5.0. Users can skip setup.
User Value: Captures the player context needed for future game, court, and club recommendations while keeping entry friction low.
Implementation Detail: OnboardingScreen uses local step state; skill selection backed by FormTierPicker + lib/skillTiers; can open DuprExplainerSheet for context.
Status: Active (uses new form library + DUPR explainer sheet)
Current Status Notes: UI-only; onboarding values are not persisted and reset with app state.

Included Features: Personalized home; quick actions; featured game; streak card
Description: Shows the default post-login dashboard with greeting, quick action tiles, a featured game, discoverable game cards, and progress/streak messaging.
User Value: Gives players a fast daily starting point and routes them into the highest-value workflows.
Implementation Detail: HomeScreen uses quickActions and demoGames; actions navigate to Nearby, Games, Clubs, Create Game, and Game Details.
Current Status Notes: Static demo content with wired screen navigation.

Included Features: Game listings; date browsing; game filter sheet
Description: Allows users to browse playable sessions through game cards / GameRow, My/Upcoming/Completed segmented control, horizontal date chips, and the GameFilterSheet bottom sheet.
User Value: Helps players find games that match timing, skill level, format, distance, and interest.
Implementation Detail: GamesScreen uses myGames, upcomingGames, completedGames, dateChips, and tab state. GameFilterSheet opens as bottom sheet and emits onApply.
Status: Active (filter is now bottom sheet from 2026-05-27)
Current Status Notes: Static game arrays. Date/filter selections are visual/local and are not applied to real queries.

Included Features: Game profile; join/leave; game chat
Description: Presents full game context including schedule, skill range, player count, organizer, court, description, participants, join button, and chat composer.
User Value: Converts discovery into participation and supports lightweight pre-game coordination.
Implementation Detail: GameDetailsScreen receives optional gameId, renders static detail content, and toggles local joined state. Chat messages and input are presentational.
Current Status Notes: Detail screen is wired but data is fixed. Join state and chat are local-only with no participant or messaging backend.

Included Features: Create game form; post-create actions
Description: Lets organizers define a new session with title, court, date/time, skill range (FormTierPicker), player limit, visibility, and description, then choose View Game or Invite Players.
User Value: Supports user-generated play sessions and a natural follow-up path for inviting players.
Main Screens: Create Game, Invite Players, Game Details
Implementation Detail: CreateGameScreen uses useForm + FormField/FormSelect/FormTierPicker. Success actions navigate to game-details or invite-players using placeholder ID new. Triggered from TabBar Create (no longer FAB).
Current Status Notes: Local-only; no saved game record, validation service, or returned backend ID.

Included Features: Suggested player selection; share link; send invites
Description: Displays a share-link card and selectable suggested players before showing an invite success state.
User Value: Helps organizers seed attendance and share new games beyond passive discovery.
Implementation Detail: InvitePlayersScreen tracks selected players in component state and routes back to Game Details after sending.
Current Status Notes: Static suggested players. No invite delivery, clipboard integration, or backend mutation confirmed.

Included Features: Nearby list; map view; locate control; court filter sheet
Description: Supports court browsing through list/map modes (Segmented), quick filter chips, a Leaflet map, browser geolocation locate control, and the NearbyFilterSheet bottom sheet.
User Value: Helps players find nearby places to play based on distance, access, surface, amenities, and location.
Implementation Detail: NearbyScreen uses demoCourts, local activeTab, local viewMode (Segmented), Austin demo coordinates, and Leaflet map.locate(). NearbyFilterSheet opens as bottom sheet.
Status: Active (filter is now bottom sheet from 2026-05-27)
Current Status Notes: Static Austin court data. Map frontend works with demo coordinates; filters are UI-only.

Included Features: Venue profile; directions; related games
Description: Shows venue hero, address, rating, favorite/share actions, amenities, directions, and games this week (GameRow).
User Value: Helps users evaluate a court and move from venue discovery to actual play.
Implementation Detail: CourtDetailsScreen renders static venue content, opens a Google Maps placeholder, and routes to Games or Game Details.
Current Status Notes: Static detail. Receives optional courtId but does not fetch by ID.

Included Features: My clubs; discover clubs; create club; club details
Description: Covers club browsing, featured club discovery, join CTAs, club creation, and a club detail view with About, Events, Members, and Chat tabs.
User Value: Helps players find recurring communities and start new groups around shared play preferences.
Implementation Detail: ClubsScreen uses myClubs and discoverClubs; CreateClubScreen uses local multi-step form state; ClubDetailsScreen uses local activeTab.
Current Status Notes: Static club/member/message data. Join/create behavior is presentational or local-only.

Included Features: Cross-entity search; recent searches; categorized results
Description: Provides full-screen search across courts, games, clubs, and players, including recent search chips and result categories.
User Value: Lets users jump directly to known entities or resume recent discovery paths.
Implementation Detail: SearchScreen uses local query state and static result catalogs; result clicks route by entity type.
Current Status Notes: Static results. No search API, debounce, loading, empty, or error states.

Included Features: Notification inbox; mark all read; notification click-through
Description: Shows invites, joins, reminders, and club updates with read/unread styling and a mark-all-read action.
User Value: Keeps users aware of activity and lets them act on updates.
Implementation Detail: NotificationsScreen initializes from initialNotifications; markAllRead updates local state; item clicks can navigate to related screens.
Current Status Notes: Static initial notifications. Read state is local and not durable.

Included Features: Player profile; edit profile; account/settings menu
Description: Provides player identity, skill/location summary, stats, edit profile form (now using FormField/FormTierPicker), settings sections, support links, notifications link, and logout.
User Value: Gives users a central account hub for identity, preferences, and session control.
Implementation Detail: ProfileScreen shows static Riley Pickler data; EditProfileScreen uses useForm + FormField/FormTierPicker; SettingsScreen renders static section definitions; logout returns to Landing.
Status: Active (uses form library; logout to Landing from 2026-05-27)
Current Status Notes: Mostly static/local. Some settings rows may not map to implemented screens.

Included Features: Install prompt; service worker auto-update
Description: Supports installable app behavior and checks for updated service worker builds with a reload guard.
User Value: Improves mobile app-like access and keeps installed builds current.
Implementation Detail: InstallPrompt handles browser install prompt/iOS copy; pwaUpdate.ts registers virtual:pwa-register, checks on pageshow/visibility/load, and polls every 30 minutes.
Current Status Notes: Wired globally. Dismissal is local to component state.

Included Features: Offline banner; demo offline mode
Description: Top-of-app banner appears when the browser reports offline or when the demo offline mode is selected.
User Value: Reassures users that the app knows it is offline rather than appearing broken.
Implementation Detail: OfflineBanner subscribes to online/offline events; DemoStateProvider can force the offline state for review.
Current Status Notes: No offline write queue yet; reads still need cached responses to be useful.

Included Features: Runtime switch between normal / empty / loading / error / offline
Description: A globally mounted DemoStateControl widget lets reviewers toggle the app state to inspect failure surfaces without code changes.
User Value: Lets stakeholders see what every failure mode looks like in seconds.
Implementation Detail: DemoStateProvider in App.tsx wraps all children; DemoStateControl exposes the state switcher.
Current Status Notes: Should be gated behind an env flag for production builds.

Included Features: Theme application
Description: Applies and reacts to the current theme at the document root.
User Value: Foundation for future dark mode / accessibility themes.
Implementation Detail: useTheme hook runs in AppInner and applies tokens at root.
Current Status Notes: Theme switcher UI not yet exposed to users.

Included Features: Single source of truth for skill levels
Description: Centralized skill tier definitions and helpers used by onboarding and profile.
User Value: Consistent tier labels and ranges across the app.
Implementation Detail: lib/skillTiers.ts feeds FormTierPicker.

Included Features: OS reduce-motion preference
Description: Animated components opt out when the OS prefers reduced motion.
User Value: Accessibility for motion-sensitive users.
Implementation Detail: usePrefersReducedMotion hook in animated components.
